frameworks.name: rate-distortion theory
frameworks.one_line: Optimal lossy channel that minimizes perceptual error under fixed information capacity

frameworks.name: capacity-limited perception
frameworks.one_line: Frames perception as information transmission with hard channel constraints

frameworks.name: computational-level analysis
frameworks.one_line: Marr-level normative account of what perception optimizes

core_question: What constitutes an optimal perceptual system when information must be lost under capacity limits?
key_assumptions: Perception serves behavioral objectives via lossy information transmission
Biological processing imposes a strong constraint on transmittable information
Some information must be discarded in the act of perception
Perceptual error has a cost that can be traded against rate
manipulations: Comparison of capacity-limited regimes across benchmark domains
key_findings: Rate-distortion models produce quantitatively precise fits to human perceptual performance
Framework applies across absolute identification and visual working memory as benchmark capacity-limited domains
Optimal trade-off between communication cost and distortion explains perceptual behavior
interpretations: Rate-distortion theory offers a general, principled computational-level framework for perception
Modeling perception as optimal lossy coding yields new insights into the nature and goals of perception
